title: jacquemontia lasioclados
intro: Jacquemontia lasioclados, commonly known as Beach Morning Glory, is a tropical plant species that is native to the coastal regions of the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties and has been found to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infections. The therapeutic actions of Jacquemontia lasioclados include antioxidant, antipyretic, and antispasmodic activities, which can help in reducing fever, relieving muscle spasms, and protecting cells from oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been shown to have potential in treating various health conditions, including cardiovascular diseases and cancer.
benefits: Jacquemontia lasioclados has many benefits, such as its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression due to its high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids. This plant has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the body, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Jacquemontia lasioclados has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and heart disease. By incorporating Jacquemontia lasioclados into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a better quality of life and increased energy levels.

intro_study: According to "Bioorganic chemistry", jacquemontia lasioclados benefits from its extracts, particularly the floral extract and the compound isoorientin (3), which have shown strong antioxidant and cytotoxic effects, indicating potential application in pharmaceutical industries.

parts: Jacquemontia lasioclados has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves of Jacquemontia lasioclados contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of the plant are rich in phenolic compounds and essential oils, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever and digestive problems. The roots of Jacquemontia lasioclados contain alkaloids, which are believed to have analgesic and antiseptic properties.
side_effects: Jacquemontia lasioclados can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. Prolonged use of the plant has also been linked to an increased risk of liver damage and kidney dysfunction, particularly when consumed in large quantities. In rare cases, ingestion of the plant can lead to more severe symptoms, including seizures, confusion, and respiratory distress. It is essential to consult with a medical professional or a qualified herbalist before using Jacquemontia lasioclados for medicinal purposes.
